name: nRF BlueNullifier 2 — nRF24L01 [LAB-ONLY / illegal to operate]
label: nRF BlueNullifier 2 — nRF24L01 [LAB-ONLY / illegal to operate]
description: wirebits/nrfBlueNullifier (GPL-3.0), variant nrfBlueNullifier-2-nrf24L01: a NodeMCU-32S (ESP32-WROOM-32) that drives 2x nRF24L01+ radios in continuous constant-carrier at max PA while hopping channels — a 2.4 GHz noise transmitter (jammer). *** LAB-ONLY / ILLEGAL TO OPERATE (FCC 47 U.S.C. 333). *** Cyber Controller FLASHES the pinned, SHA-256-verified image for study only. This firmware has NO serial interface (no Serial.begin, no command parser — it jams on power-up); CC exposes NO operate/transmit control and there is nothing on the wire to drive. Its only 'control' is the physical power switch.

firmware_urls.note: Prebuilt bins are committed in the repo tree (no GitHub Release). Fetched pinned to commit 1dfc4d3 from raw.githubusercontent.com and SHA-256-verified at flash time, never vendored.

note: esptool offsets bootloader@0x1000 / partitions@0x8000 / app@0x10000, NO boot_app0 @0xE000 (the repo ships only these 3 bins, same single-app flash set as BlueJammer-V2). Wire 2x nRF24L01+ (HSPI + VSPI) with a 10uF cap per module VCC/GND (brownout fix). CC flashes + verifies only — it never operates the transmitter and there is no serial control surface to operate.

assets.name: nrfBlueNullifier-2-nrf24L01.bin
assets.label: nRF BlueNullifier 2 ESP32 app image [LAB-ONLY / illegal to operate]
